channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Global geopolitical news headlines curated by Frontion

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump fires Navy Secretary John Phelan amid ongoing Iran war
item.description: Pentagon announced Navy Secretary John Phelan will leave office immediately, making him the latest in a string of senior military officials dismissed during the US-Iran conflict.
item.dc:creator: Defense News

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran fires on three ships in Strait of Hormuz as ceasefire holds
item.description: Iran state TV reported the Revolutionary Guard attacked two ships which are now in Iranian custody, extending the blockade of the vital waterway.
item.dc:creator: SCMP

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK PM Starmer refuses to resign amid growing Mandelson scandal
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer faced fresh calls to step down over his appointment of Peter Mandelson, defending his decision in Parliament.
item.dc:creator: SCMP

item.title: 🇨🇳🇹🇼 US slams China after African countries block Taiwan president's eSwatini trip
item.description: A State Department official called the incident an abuse of the international civil aviation system after African nations prevented Taiwan President Tsai Ing-wen from transiting.
item.dc:creator: SCMP

item.title: ⚔️ War in Iran has depleted half of US Patriot and THAAD missile stockpiles
item.description: The prolonged conflict with Iran has consumed significant portions of American air defense reserves, raising concerns about global military readiness.
item.dc:creator: Kyiv Independent

item.title: ⚓ Pentagon replaces Navy Secretary amid Iran blockade
item.description: US Navy Secretary John C Phelan is leaving "effective immediately" and being replaced by Undersecretary Hung Cao, with the Pentagon providing no explanation for the sudden leadership change during the ongoing Iran crisis.
item.dc:creator: BBC

item.title: 🇺🇸 Two Americans killed in Mexico crash were CIA officers
item.description: Two Americans killed in a vehicle crash in Chihuahua, Mexico on Sunday were CIA officers returning from a counter-cartel operation led by Mexican armed forces. The incident raises questions about US-Mexico security cooperation.
item.dc:creator: New York Times

item.title: 🇲🇽 Migrant caravan leaves southern Mexico, many no longer US-bound
item.description: A migrant caravan departed from a southern Mexican city, but participants say many are no longer aiming for the US border, reflecting shifting migration patterns amid heightened enforcement.
item.dc:creator: AP News

item.title: 💰 Condom prices could surge 30% due to Iran war
item.description: Karex, the world's largest condom producer which supplies brands like Durex and Trojan, warns prices could rise 30% as the US-Iran conflict disrupts global supply chains.
item.dc:creator: The Guardian

item.title: ⚖️ El Salvador holds mass trial for 486 alleged MS-13 members
item.description: El Salvador conducted a mass trial for 486 alleged members of the notorious MS-13 gang, with human rights groups warning that collective prosecutions violate due process and block defendants from accessing legal counsel.
item.dc:creator: The Guardian

item.title: ✈️⛽ Lufthansa Cuts 20,000 Summer Flights as Iran War Drives Jet Fuel Prices Higher
item.description: Germany's Lufthansa will eliminate 20,000 European short-haul flights this summer as jet fuel prices doubled since the US-Iran war began. The carrier is closing its CityLine service and canceling routes to 10 cities as the Strait of Hormuz disruption threatens Europe's fuel supply.
item.dc:creator: BBC

item.title: 🇺🇦 Moldova's former most powerful person sentenced to 19 years in jail
item.description: Vladimir Plahotniuc, once Moldova's wealthiest businessman and de facto ruler of the country in the 2010s, received a 19-year sentence in absentia. His fall from grace is seen as part of Moldova's alignment with European democratic values.
item.dc:creator: Kyiv Independent

item.title: 🇩🇪 Germany unveils strategy to become Europe's strongest military by 2039
item.description: German Defense Minister Boris Pistorius announced Germany's first military strategy since WWII, moving away from rigid hardware quotas toward flexible, effects-based planning to counter Russia's growing threat.
item.dc:creator: Defense News

item.title: ⚠️ Russia could be ready for NATO conflict year after Ukraine, Dutch warn
item.description: Dutch intelligence warns Moscow's goal would not be to defeat NATO militarily, but to divide the alliance through limited territorial gains, according to a new report.
item.dc:creator: Defense News

item.title: 🇨🇦 Canada PM hits back after Trump team demands 'entry fee' for trade talks
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney pushed back after reports that Trump's team demanded payments before engaging in trade negotiations.
item.dc:creator: SCMP

item.title: 🇮🇷 Baltic nations brace for impact of Iran war delaying US weapons shipments
item.description: Estonia, Latvia and Lithuania fear delays in promised US weapons deliveries as the Pentagon prioritizes the Middle East conflict, leaving them vulnerable to potential Russian aggression.
item.dc:creator: Defense News

item.title: 🌍 US-Iran ceasefire holds as both sides maintain blockades
item.description: The US and Iran maintained a fragile ceasefire while both kept blockades in place. Trump extended the ceasefire amid continuing tensions over the Strait of Hormuz and ongoing negotiations.
item.dc:creator: AP News

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK sends warships to South Atlantic for first time since Falklands
item.description: Britain is deploying warships to the South Atlantic for the first time since the Falklands War amid growing concerns about Argentine militarization and regional tensions.
item.dc:creator: BBC

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump administration opens investigation into CIA over Russia probe origins
item.description: The Trump Justice Department is investigating former CIA Director John Brennan and other officials over the origins of the Russia investigation, reopening questions about the 2016 election interference probe.
item.dc:creator: AP News

item.title: 🌊 NATO responds to suspected sabotage of deep-sea cable
item.description: NATO launched its first-ever response to suspected undersea cable sabotage, marking a significant escalation in Western efforts to protect critical infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Wall Street Journal

item.title: 🇨🇩 Rwanda-backed rebels enter Congo safe haven city
item.description: Rwanda-backed M23 rebels entered Goma, a key humanitarian hub in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo, threatening hundreds of thousands of displaced civilians.
item.dc:creator: Wall Street Journal

item.title: 🇧🇷 Brazil's Lula says Trump 'lying' about Venezuela operation
item.description: Brazilian President Lula da Silva accused Donald Trump of lying about the nature of US military operations in Venezuela, deepening diplomatic tensions between Washington and Latin America.
item.dc:creator: BBC

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iranian Parliament Speaker blames Trump's blockade for diplomatic impasse
item.description: Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf said the US and Israel will not achieve their goals "through bullying" as the fragile truce continues.
item.dc:creator: BBC

item.title: 🇺🇸 Pentagon delays weapons deliveries to European allies due to Iran war
item.description: The US has informed European allies that some weapons deliveries will be delayed as the Iran conflict strains American military stockpiles.
item.dc:creator: BBC

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel jails soldiers who smashed Jesus statue in Lebanon
item.description: Critics say Israeli attacks on Christian sites challenge claims of a Judeo-Christian shared heritage and mutual respect.
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
